visualGoal: Make a would-be sailor feel that VR is the one path that skips the whole marine hardware problem — hands are the helm — while being honest that it's sailing-only and the headset is the real cost. This is a LOW-product-density explainer: prove the single relevant product (Meta Quest 3) with a verified photo, then carry the page on cost/scorecard tables, a numbered buy order, and comfort/training callouts rather than a product grid. Do NOT force product cards.

realProductPhotosNeeded.product: Meta Touch controllers (as VR sailing tiller/sheet input)
realProductPhotosNeeded.notes: Article makes a controllers-vs-hand-tracking point (controllers for racing precision, hands for skill-building). Currently text/tip-callout only because no verified in-context photo. Controllers ship in-box, so this is a supporting visual, not a product card. Low priority.

editorialCompositePrompts.prompt: Editorial 16:9 cover: a person seated in a swivel chair wearing a VR headset, gloved-look hands reaching out as if gripping an invisible tiller and sheet line, a teal ocean horizon and sail silhouette suggested as soft ambient reflection, warm rim light, calm not frantic, leave clean overlay space for a real Meta Quest 3 product cut-out. No fake branded hardware, no fake UI, no readable fake logos.
editorialCompositePrompts.negativePrompt: fake logos, fake headset brand marks, warped hands, extra fingers, impossible ergonomics, tiny unreadable text, procedural gradient art, seasick-green cliche

infographicPrompts.title: What VR sailing actually costs in 2026
content: NauticEd Self Mastery + Nav Rules FREE
Sailing Pass + Docking module = optional paid upgrades
infographicPrompts.style: IgnitionSim command-center chart, large readable text, marine teal/gold palette, real Quest 3 photo as the anchor row marker for Codex overlay. Label prices as of April 2026 Meta pricing.

infographicPrompts.title: VR sailing vs. a physical helm rig
content: VR wins: no hardware, hand-tracked tiller/sheet, NauticEd training, cross-VR reuse
Physical wins: engine/throttle realism, long-session comfort, multi-monitor charting/ECDIS, lower up-front cost
Decision hinge: are you a SAILOR or a SHIP HANDLER?
infographicPrompts.style: Two-column comparison, marine palette, clear win/lose iconography, no star ratings, no brand ranking

infographicPrompts.title: Beat VR sailing motion sickness
content: Sit in a swivel chair (turn with the boat)
Sea legs in about a week
infographicPrompts.style: Numbered ladder, calm marine palette, reassuring not alarming (this is a comfort tip, keep it boring/helpful)

curatorScenePrompts.prompt: Realistic adult marine-sim curator Gus Calder in a tidy home study, VR headset resting on the desk beside a nautical chart and a coffee, one hand gesturing as if explaining the tiller motion, practical and grounded not fantasy, warm daylight, 4:5 portrait, leave space for a caption.

screenshotsOrGameMediaNeeded.need: A MarineVerse Sailing Club in-headset gameplay still showing hands on the tiller/sheet with a boat heeling and a course mark ahead — proves the 'your hands are the helm' claim and the spatial wind/telltale point.
screenshotsOrGameMediaNeeded.sourceHint: MarineVerse press kit / Meta store listing media (rights-check before use).

screenshotsOrGameMediaNeeded.need: A screenshot of the NauticEd Training Room module inside MarineVerse (Self Mastery or Docking) to back the 'real course integration' claim.
screenshotsOrGameMediaNeeded.sourceHint: NauticEd VR sailing course page / MarineVerse media (rights-check).

openQuestions: True non-sale base price of MarineVerse Sailing Club? Sources diverge ($19.99 NauticEd vs $29.99 QuestStoreDB base). Article says '~$10-30, check current' to stay safe.
Sailing Pass model: QuestStoreDB shows a 12-month pass ($99.99/$49.99), but marine.json says '~$13/month' — reconcile monthly vs annual and fix marine.json.
marine.json Quest priceNote ('$499-649') is stale after Meta's April 2026 increase ($599.99 for 512GB). Update the product JSON priceNote in a follow-up pass.
